task_completion.description: Verify that case 0001.vital is opened in Vital Recorder
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vital Recorder application window is visible with 'case 0001.vital' loaded, showing physiological waveforms or track lists.
A file named 'case_0001_data.csv' is visible on the Desktop or within a file explorer window navigating to C:\Users\Docker\Desktop.

task_completion.description: Verify that case 0002.vital is opened in Vital Recorder
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vital Recorder application window is visible with 'case 0002.vital' loaded, showing physiological waveforms or track lists.
A file named 'case_0002_data.csv' is visible on the Desktop or within a file explorer window navigating to C:\Users\Docker\Desktop.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the comparison summary text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'case_comparison.txt' is visible on the Desktop or open in a text editor.

task_completion.description: Verify the comparison text file documents the required differences (duration, tracks, timeline)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'case_comparison.txt' are visible in an editor, explicitly comparing Case 0001 and Case 0002 regarding their recording durations, available physiological tracks, and surgical event timelines (case start, surgery start, surgery finish, case finish).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Vital Recorder GUI to export the data
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with Vital Recorder's export menus or dialog boxes, rather than using terminal scripts to convert the .vital files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a text editor to write the summary
integrity.visual_evidence: The file 'case_comparison.txt' should be seen open in a standard GUI text editor (like Notepad), rather than being generated solely via terminal echo commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually inspected the case data in the application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent actively viewing the tracks and timeline/event information within the Vital Recorder interface for both cases before writing the summary.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Case 0001 duration is 3h 12m 22s and contains tracks for arterial pressure (ART), multiple ECG leads, pulse oximetry (PLETH), capnography (CO2), and sevoflurane. Case 0002 duration is 4h 22m 21s and contains tracks for ECG, pulse oximetry, heart rate, ST segment monitoring, and ventilator parameters. The summary text should reflect these specific differences.
